chapter_context.summary: Prophesies the gathering of all pagan nations for judgment in the Valley of Jehoshaphat for their scattering and abuse of God's people, issuing a famous ironic call to beat plowshares into swords for war, culminating in cosmic disruptions and the final peace, fertility, and cleansing of Judah.

outline.text: Gathering of the Nations in the Valley of Jehoshaphat; Recompense on Tyre, Sidon, and Philistia for Exploitation
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Gathering of the Nations in the Valley of Jehoshaphat; Recompense on Tyre, Sidon, and Philistia for Exploitation (3:1-8), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Joel.

outline.text: The Ironic Proclamation of War: Plowshares into Swords, the Valley of Decision, and the Lord Roaring from Zion
outline.explanation: This section focuses on The Ironic Proclamation of War: Plowshares into Swords, the Valley of Decision, and the Lord Roaring from Zion (3:9-17), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Joel.

outline.text: Final Blessings: Mountains Dropping Sweet Wine, a Fountain from the House of the Lord, and Cleansing of Bloodguilt
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Final Blessings: Mountains Dropping Sweet Wine, a Fountain from the House of the Lord, and Cleansing of Bloodguilt (3:18-21), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Joel.

key_themes.title: The Day of the Lord
key_themes.description: A central prophetic theme denoting a time of God's direct, sovereign intervention in human history for judgment and salvation.

key_themes.title: Genuine Inward Repentance
key_themes.description: Joel emphasizes that external rituals (garment-rending) are useless without sincere change of heart and turning to God.

devotional.question: Reflecting on the warnings and invitations of Chapter 3, what does it mean to rend your heart rather than your garments in your walk with God?
How does the promise of the outpouring of the Holy Spirit and cosmic salvation in this chapter bring hope and confidence to your daily faith?

cultural_historical.title: Locust Plagues in the Ancient Near East
cultural_historical.text: Swarms of locusts were a frequent and devastating threat to ancient agrarian economies, capable of stripping entire regions of vegetation in hours, which Joel uses as a powerful metaphor for invading armies and divine judgment.
